steps.title: Use Desmos to fit the table
steps.content: The table represents a line, so the fastest path is to enter the points and let Desmos find the equation. Use $x_1=[-6,6]$ and $y_1=[65,29]$, then type $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=-3$ and $b=47$, so the line is $y=-3x+47$.

steps.title: Match the Desmos line to standard form
steps.content: Rewrite $y=-3x+47$ so it looks like the choices: add $3x$ to both sides to get $3x+y=47$, then multiply by 3 to get $9x+3y=141$. That is choice A.

steps.title: Evaluate all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '9x+3y=141': Equivalent to 3x+y=47, slope = -3. Passes through (-6,65) and (6,29). ✓
  • Choice B — '9x+3y=3': Equivalent to 3x+y=1, y-intercept 1. Does not match the table. ✗
  • Choice C — '3x+9y=141': Equivalent to x+3y=47, slope = -1/3. Does not match slope -3. ✗
  • Choice D — '3x+9y=3': Equivalent to x+3y=1, slope = -1/3 and wrong intercept. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
